title: Khap-Salung
content:
Seeing that I adore you,
    Scarf of golden flowers,
    Why do you stay unmarried?
    As the liana at a tree's foot
    That quivers to wind it round,
    So do I wait for you. I pray you
    Do not detest me....

    I have come to say farewell.
    Farewell, scarf;
    Garden Royal
    Where none may enter,
    Gaudy money
    I may not spend.

    Song of the Love Nights of Laos.
meaning:
"Khap-Salung" is a quintessential example of Edward Powys Mathers (As Translator)'s signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
